item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No other article was able to put the word "noodle" into an SCP successfully. Well done. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
JON WHERE IS MY LASAGNA

- the g a r f

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
how

in the heck

did you do that

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nope, the president is long gone. They had to go through the special "Leifman Protocol" to essentially erase all memory of this particular president (and anyone else that went missing) existing. It's why so many names are redacted in the SCP. :]

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can't tell if they found the president or not. If they didn't, I can't imagine the hoops the Foundation would have to go through to fix that.

item.title: incredible
item.content:encoded: I love how you made this look so amazing, this was a great read. You did an excellent job writing this and making it look great. The logs were interesting and kept me hooked the entire time reading them. Great work!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
How did you even

How

How

How

How did you make the article look this amazing? It's one of the best formated articles I have seen in a very, very, very long time. Amazing job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh my God, you just killed me.
2105 is one of your favorites? That is so kind of you to say!

And thank you for the correction. I'll fix it as soon as I get to a computer.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The day prior, Dr. Lark left spaghetti noodles near SCP-4378 to this interview spelling "Hello?"

Is this supposed to say:

The day prior to this interview, Dr. Lark left spaghetti noodles near SCP-4378 spelling "Hello?"

Aside from that, I enjoyed this and I'm really glad to see the author of one of my favorite skips back on the site. I feel like the site's been having a recent influx of former members returning.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What do you mean, friend?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Same person who did everything else of significance in this skip. A bunch of black rectangles.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks to you! :D

I appreciate all the input and advice. The open in particular was made significantly better due to you so I am very grateful.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good to see this up and do well!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's a creepy pasta.

Oh my god, I legitimately did not think of that…
Just gonna… file that one away…

Anyway, I'm happy you hesitantly enjoyed it. :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ah, I get it. It's a creepy pasta.

Normally not a fan of goofy SCPs, but this one has enough intrigue to get a hesitant upvote. Though I'm still unsure how I feel about the White House setting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you very much!

I will look over that section and give it a once over. I think I know what it may be.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've fixed several of the grammar mistakes for you. If I had one last nitpick, it would be the "Note from Project Lead Dr. Lark" section - I don't know why, but the writing quality of that particular part feels a lot lower than the rest of the article. Maybe revise that bit a few more times?

Other than that, I hope this article starts gaining more popularity soon. It's very enjoyable.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's honestly impressive.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
b. e. a. utiful

I'm not sure I even want to ask how you made that lol

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: no way, graphic design is my passion too!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks! My passion (and my actual IRL job) is graphic design.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The whole White House setting felt bizarre (not in a good way) as there is no thematic connection between the White House and noodles

The implication is that there is a sentient being (essentially the Flying Spaghetti Monster) that is trying to assert its influence in to our world. It intentionally chose the White House. I was hoping the final interview would hint at that.

As for being derivative of other articles, I'm not entirely sure what you are talking about? The only other article I am aware of similar to the Noodleverse is SCP-503-ARC.

With the noodle thing, I wrote that part 2 years ago and I can't really remember my reasoning for it. It's not particularly important, though, it was just meant to be an odd thing to add flavor. :P

I am sorry, in the end, you did not enjoy it. There's always next time. :]

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is an amusing idea, but I ended up downvoting. The core of a noodle that must be handled very carefully to prevent chaos is good, but there is a lot of additional fluff that sends the tone all over the place (as Cimmerian already said) and also felt derivative of other articles - the infinite noodle dimension and the bad-English interview logs in particular. The whole White House setting felt bizarre (not in a good way) as there is no thematic connection between the White House and noodles, it comes across as a hamfisted way to make this as troublesome as possible without justification, and is really the sort of thing that would lead to a Broken Masquerade canon, which has a particular feel this isn't going for. The weird grammar issues are also present (mostly strange capitalisation choices).

On a lighter note, the noodle designations are questionable (couscous? really?), as are the non-noodle designations (I wouldn't really consider farfalle to be a noodle, but it has about the same level of noodle-related properties as spätzle)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
We've talked at length about this, but I am sorry you did not like it.

I disagree personally with most of what you said most of it would just be a repeat of our conversation we've already had.

The article does not seem to be for you. Maybe my next one will be.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: nice job on that photoshop

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a -1 for me. No hesitation.

The idea here seems like it could work but the execution is a bit of a mess.

I don't feel anything really coherent about the way this is presented. I come into this immediately knowing what to expect out of it and it goes exactly as I imagined it would. There's no natural raising of stakes.

Those stakes are almost as high as they can be from the outset (with the secretary of state thing) and there's no subversion of expectations. That ruins any sort of coherent narrative arc when the piece is clearly trying to present a story.

I also don't get a strong sense of the thematic tone you're aiming for. Is this serious? Is it silly? It tries to be both and as a result only hits on either tone weakly. The starkly contrasting themes actually just makes the lack of good clinical tone throughout more apparent. I can see bits that are more silly that just don't really use good clinical language alongside bits that are serious and actually mostly hit the mark.

On top of that, there's insertion of interview logs with the pasta that do not seem to help develop anything in your story and aren't really good enough to work in the piece on their own.

Not to mention that there's blackboxing in the Containment procedures and while I have a personal opinion about overuse of blackbox that I am willing to put to the side most of the time that is something I cannot abide.

Good luck with your article!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really appreciate if! That was the idea haha

Let me know of any errors you find and I can correct them, or feel free to do so yourself.

Thanks for the kind words!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There are a few grammatical errors and odd word choices throughout, but that doesn't distract from how great this is. It's absurd, it's goofy, and it somehow manages to become genuinely unsettling through it all. I laughed out loud when I finished reading the Special Containment Procedures only to scroll down to the description and find out I'm reading about a single uncooked noodle.

+1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you so much! You don't know how good that is to hear.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Love it, it mixes just the right amount of interesting wacky and funny together. Keep up the good work

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Cogito, ergo sum pasta.

Her noodly appendage reaches out to thank:

DrBleep
Modern_Erasmus
Leveritas
The Great Hippo
Doctor Cimmerian
Lazar Lyusternik
DiamondInTheMaking

Hopefully that is everyone!

I started this monster back in 2016 with the idea of making a "Goofy" object that actually causes a ton of chaos with a minor sliver of horror. It kinda snowballed into this crazy thing you see now. I hope it's a fun read! It was definitely fun to write (even if it took forever.)

The top photo is a photoshop by me that is made up of these two images:
Image 1 - Public Domain
Image 2 - CC0 Creative Commons

The bottom image is created by myself using Audacity and Sonic Visualizer.

Also, I leave you with a final question… Who caused the earthquake?
